title: tragia nepetifolia var. ramosa
intro: Tragia nepetifolia var. ramosa, also known as the branched spurge, is a plant species in the family Euphorbiaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin problems, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and analgesic properties. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing. Tragia nepetifolia var. ramosa can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or poultices, which can be applied topically or consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
